title: A clearer map, but not stronger evidence for LEV yet
summary: We can now see the major longevity research tracks side by side. That is real progress. But it does not yet mean LEV is close. The main missing proof is still repeated human studies showing that people function better for longer.
where_we_are_now: The field is in early human testing. Some tracks now have early biomarker results, functional results, disease results, or trials worth watching. Most are still uncertain, narrow, or only indirectly about aging itself.
what_changed_recently: In June, the tracker finished first-pass public summaries for all 38 research tracks and began adding missing context. The story changed from a partial map to a working map. It did not give us a new reason to be more optimistic about LEV.

journey_steps.label: Before
journey_steps.title: Scattered examples
journey_steps.summary: Longevity research looked like a set of isolated intervention stories. It was hard to compare which areas were moving and which were mostly promise.

journey_steps.label: Now
journey_steps.title: A working map
journey_steps.summary: All 38 research tracks now have public outlooks. Readers can compare stage, confidence, blockers, and recent movement across the hallmarks.

journey_steps.label: Next
journey_steps.title: The human proof gap
journey_steps.summary: The next major step is repeated human evidence that people function better for longer, with safety and lasting benefit clear enough to matter.

progress_moments.label: The map is now complete enough to compare
progress_moments.summary: All 38 research tracks now have first-pass public outlooks, so readers can compare progress across hallmarks instead of reading isolated examples.
progress_moments.impact_on_outlook: That helps readers see where work is happening. It did not change the overall stage, confidence, or 2036 scenario.

progress_moments.label: Early signs in people are showing up in more places
progress_moments.summary: Stem-cell replacement, mitochondrial maintenance, nutrient sensing, microbiome restoration, inflammatory modulation, and systemic signaling now have clearer human evidence entries in the public map.
progress_moments.impact_on_outlook: The field is easier to track seriously, but many signs are still narrow, disease-specific, or mostly based on biomarkers.

progress_moments.label: Missing-context updates are keeping the forecast honest
progress_moments.summary: The transposon and somatic-mutation update added biomarker, trial, program, and biology context while keeping the track at low confidence and an early biomarker stage.
progress_moments.impact_on_outlook: The update made the evidence clearer without treating activity as proof.

watchlist.label: Human studies where people function better for longer
watchlist.summary: This is the missing proof that would move the story from interesting research to meaningful LEV progress.
watchlist.signal_to_watch: Trials that show long-lasting functional improvement in aging-relevant populations, not only biomarker movement or narrow disease response.

watchlist.label: Fast-moving but still uncertain tracks
watchlist.summary: Partial reprogramming, immune clearance of senescent cells, extracellular vesicle approaches, and microbiome restoration have visible motion but still need stronger links to useful human results and safety.
watchlist.signal_to_watch: Human data, long-lasting effects, delivery control, and clear separation between biological excitement and clinical usefulness.

watchlist.label: Updates that actually change the outlook
watchlist.summary: Some near-term progress may come from making the evidence map clearer rather than from new results. That still matters when it shows which claims are solid, thin, or overclaimed.
watchlist.signal_to_watch: Repairs that change stage, momentum, confidence, or the main blocker for a hallmark or track.

focus_priorities.label: Make evidence maps clearer before raising expectations
focus_priorities.rationale: The field has enough activity to look busy and enough weak evidence to invite overstatement. The next useful work is separating direct aging evidence from nearby disease or biomarker evidence.
focus_priorities.next_useful_work: Prioritize missing-context updates for tracks where early signs in people exist but confidence remains low.

focus_priorities.label: Watch trial results, not just new trial launches
focus_priorities.rationale: Several tracks now have active or recruiting human studies. The value comes when those studies produce clear results, safety boundaries, or clear nulls.
focus_priorities.next_useful_work: Keep trial activity linked to outcome criteria so launches do not look like proof.

focus_priorities.label: Look for gaps between lab work and useful human results
focus_priorities.rationale: Some hallmarks have many biology ideas but fewer clean intervention programs that can be tested against aging-relevant human outcomes.
focus_priorities.next_useful_work: Use the track taxonomy to identify areas with thin coverage, missing human endpoints, or unclear milestone criteria.

change_mind_items.label: Repeated human functional results
change_mind_items.summary: Several independent studies show that older adults function better for longer, not just that biomarkers move.

change_mind_items.label: Safer delivery and lasting effects
change_mind_items.summary: Fast-moving tracks show controlled delivery, acceptable safety, and benefits that last beyond short-term signals.

change_mind_items.label: Null, unsafe, or narrow human results
change_mind_items.summary: Human studies fail to show useful outcomes, reveal safety problems, or show effects that are too narrow to change the aging story.

spotlight_examples.label: Promising but uncertain
spotlight_examples.title: Microbiome metabolite restoration
spotlight_examples.summary: There are human functional signals, but the evidence is still narrow and not yet proof of broad aging benefit.

spotlight_examples.label: Fast-moving frontier
spotlight_examples.title: Partial reprogramming
spotlight_examples.summary: The biological idea could be broad, but moving it into people, controlling delivery, and proving safety remain the key questions.

spotlight_examples.label: Clear bottleneck
spotlight_examples.title: Stem-cell replacement
spotlight_examples.summary: Some human functional signal exists, but the field still has to separate general aging benefit from narrow repair stories.

revision.review_reason: Generated draft confirmed the current narrative against observed public outlook snapshots.

triggers.description: Review when a publication event after the last narrative review affects any public outlook.
Review when an overall, hallmark, or track stage, momentum, confidence, blocker, or best signal changes.
Review immediately if the 2036 scenario status changes.
Review when a new state-of-the-field edition is added or materially revised.
Review on the scheduled due date even if no material public event has occurred.
Review whenever the curator asks whether the public story still matches the evidence map.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: The field still lacks repeated human studies showing that people function better for longer in ways that clearly add up toward LEV.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: All 38 research tracks now have public first-pass outlooks, and early missing-context updates are making the evidence map clearer without giving a new reason to be more optimistic.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: Metformin is clinically familiar and mechanistically broad, but direct human geroscience evidence is still mostly biomarker, transcriptomic, and short-term pathway engagement rather than durable function or multimorbidity outcomes.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: MILES provides the main direct human biomarker anchor: short-term metformin changed glucose handling and skeletal-muscle and adipose transcriptomic pathways in older adults with impaired glucose tolerance.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: The strongest human outcomes are cardiovascular secondary-prevention results in high-risk disease populations, not broad healthy-aging or LEV-relevant rejuvenation outcomes.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: CANTOS showed that IL-1beta inhibition can reduce recurrent cardiovascular events without lipid lowering in post-MI patients with residual inflammatory risk.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: The most direct aging and healthspan evidence remains animal evidence from MSC-derived EV and conditioned-medium studies.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: Human split-face and meta-analytic dermatology evidence suggests stem-cell conditioned media or exosome-containing solutions can move skin-aging appearance and instrumented biomarker endpoints.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: No direct treated-human aging trial was identified for engineered or checkpoint-based immune clearance of senescent cells.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: Anti-uPAR CAR T cells cleared senescent cells and restored tissue homeostasis in mouse pathology models, then improved exercise capacity, metabolic function, and intestinal regeneration phenotypes in aged mice.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: The human functional rating is narrow and rests mainly on urolithin A muscle endurance or strength signals, not replicated durable frailty, mobility, disease, or mortality outcomes.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: Urolithin A has randomized human trials with muscle endurance or strength signals, mitochondrial or inflammatory biomarker movement, and a 2024 systematic review summarizing both the signal and the lack of broader physical-function benefit.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: The highest-quality human signal is structured exercise, not a druglike mitochondrial-biogenesis or dynamics therapy.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: Supervised HIIT and combined training improved older-adult aerobic capacity, while HIIT increased skeletal-muscle mitochondrial respiration, mitochondrial protein synthesis, and mitochondrial protein abundance.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: The strongest human intervention signal is TRIIM, a nine-participant uncontrolled male pilot with epigenetic-age and immunosenescence biomarkers rather than randomized functional, disease, or mortality endpoints.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: TRIIM reported lower DNA-methylation epigenetic age and immunosenescence-marker movement after one year in nine healthy men.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: Human translation is still far behind the narrative intensity.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: The mechanism could be unusually broad if it survives translation.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: The strongest direct aging-relevant signal is laromestrocel in frailty, but allogeneic MSC benefit may reflect paracrine, immunovascular, or tissue-support effects rather than durable stem-cell replacement.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: A 2026 randomized phase 2b frailty trial reported clinically meaningful 6-minute walk improvement after allogeneic MSC therapy, with a possible TIE2 biomarker response.

observed_outlook_states.blocker: The direct transposon-control intervention evidence is still mostly animal, preprint, open-label human biomarker, or no-results trial-watch evidence; no controlled human aging-function trial has shown benefit from reverse transcriptase inhibition.
observed_outlook_states.best_signal: SIRT6 and senescence studies give coherent LINE1 suppression biology, including evidence that SIRT6 repression fails with stress or age and that LINE1 cDNA can drive type I interferon activation.
